L. 92–318, title I, § 161(b)(1)86 Stat. 303June 23, 1972“The programs authorized by title VII of the Higher Education Act of 1965 [this subchapter] shall be deemed to be a continuation of the comparable programs authorized by the Higher Education Facilities Act of 1963 [former section 701 et seq. of this title].”

children.label: A
children.text: in order to attract students of superior ability and achievement, exceptional promise, and demonstrated financial need, into high-quality graduate programs and provide the students with the financial support necessary to complete advanced degrees; and

children.label: i
children.text: sustain and enhance the capacity for graduate education in areas of national need, including those areas critical to United States national and homeland security needs, such as science, technology, engineering, and mathematics; and

children.label: ii
children.text: encourage talented students to pursue scholarly careers in the humanities, social sciences, and the arts; and
